preamble: The transformation from the uvular nasal /ɴ/ to the alveolar nasal [n] is a result of place assimilation. When the moraic nasal is followed by a dental or alveolar sound, the point of articulation shifts forward to match the following consonant.

examples.note: Moraic nasal (ん) before dentals/alveolars like /t, d, n/.

sources: Vance, T. J. (2008). The Sounds of Japanese.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
